name: Fudgy Bon Bons
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces White baking bar or vanilla flavored candy coating
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a medium saucepan, combine chocolate chips and butter; cook and stir over very low heat until chips are melted and smooth.
3. Add sweetened condensed milk; mix well.
4. Lightly spoon flour into measuring cup; level off. In a medium bowl, combine flour, nuts, chocolate mixture, and vanilla; mix well.
5. Shape tablespoon-sized portions of dough around each candy kiss, covering completely. Place 1 inch apart on ungreased cookie sheets.
6. Bake in the preheated oven for 6 to 8 minutes. Cookies will be soft and appear shiny but become firm as they cool.
7. Do not over bake! Remove from cookie sheets; let cool.
8. In a small saucepan, combine white baking bar and shortening; cook and stir over low heat until melted and smooth. Drizzle over cookies.
9. Store in a tightly covered container. Makes 5 dozen cookies.
NUTRITIONAL INFORMATION: Each serving contains approximately 110 calories, 2 grams of protein, 14 grams of carbohydrates, 1 gram of fiber, 5 grams of fat (1 polyunsaturated, 3 saturated), and 6 milligrams of cholesterol. Sodium is about 20 milligrams, and potassium is around 75 milligrams. Dietary exchanges: 1 fruit serving and 1 fat serving.
Printed in Pillsbury Classic Cookbook #157, March 1994, also in the Chicago Tribune. See text about the 36th Bake-Off.
